topics.title: Preoperative Preparation in Neonates
topics.description: Neonate not feeding with aspiration risk before surgery.

decisionFlow.action: Suction upper pouch, position upright, call anesthesia support now.
Start oxygen, monitor blood gases, reassess in 5 minutes.
Warm baby, start IV fluids with electrolytes, keep NPO.

steps.title: Airway
steps.detail: Position 30-45 degrees upright and maintain continuous upper pouch suction.
steps.resourceLimited: Use frequent manual suction every 5-10 minutes if Replogle tube unavailable.

steps.title: Fluids
steps.detail: Start isotonic IV fluids with glucose and electrolyte correction.
steps.resourceLimited: Use available crystalloid and monitor urine output closely.

steps.title: Positioning
steps.detail: Avoid supine position to reduce aspiration risk.
steps.resourceLimited: Keep head elevated with rolled towel support if no pediatric bed wedge.

topics.mnemonic: WARM: Warmth, Airway, Replogle, Metabolic support
topics.parentExplanation: Your baby needs careful stabilization first so surgery is safer and recovery is smoother.
topics.mentorTip: If airway risk is unclear, treat as high risk until proven otherwise.

topics.title: Bilious Vomiting in Infant
topics.description: Green vomit requires urgent exclusion of malrotation or volvulus.

decisionFlow.action: Immediate ABC support, two IV lines, fluid bolus 10-20 mL/kg, urgent surgical review.

decisionFlow.question: Has upper GI contrast study been done?

decisionFlow.action: Keep NPO, insert NG tube, send urgent upper GI contrast to exclude volvulus.
If volvulus suspected, activate OR pathway immediately.

steps.title: Airway
steps.detail: Assess airway and breathing, provide oxygen if saturation less than 94%.
steps.resourceLimited: Use nasal oxygen and frequent reassessment when continuous monitoring is limited.

steps.title: Fluids
steps.detail: Start isotonic fluids, correct electrolytes, and monitor perfusion hourly.
steps.resourceLimited: Use available crystalloid and track capillary refill, urine output, and pulse trends.

steps.title: Decompression
steps.detail: Insert NG tube for gastric decompression and keep strict NPO.
steps.resourceLimited: If pediatric tube unavailable, use smallest safe tube and low intermittent suction.

pearls: Treat all bilious vomiting as surgical emergency until excluded
pitfalls: Waiting for repeated vomiting before escalation
topics.mnemonic: GREEN: Gastric decompression, Resuscitate, Exclude volvulus, Escalate, No feeds
topics.parentExplanation: Green vomiting can indicate bowel twisting. We must check urgently and keep your baby safe before surgery if needed.
topics.mentorTip: Time-to-imaging and time-to-OR are the key metrics. Escalate early if perfusion worsens.

topics.title: Post-op Distension
topics.description: Assess obstruction, ileus, and sepsis in the first 24 hours.

decisionFlow.question: Is there fever, tachycardia, or worsening pain?

decisionFlow.action: Start sepsis bundle, broad-spectrum antibiotics, and senior review now.

decisionFlow.question: Is NG output high with no stool/flatus?

decisionFlow.action: Urgent imaging and surgical reassessment for mechanical obstruction.
Supportive care: optimize fluids, electrolytes, pain strategy, and mobilization.

steps.title: Airway and breathing
steps.detail: Check respiratory effort and oxygen saturation; splinting pain can reduce ventilation.
steps.resourceLimited: Use bedside respiratory checks and pulse oximetry spot monitoring if continuous monitor unavailable.

steps.title: Fluids and electrolytes
steps.detail: Replace NG losses, correct potassium and chloride, chart strict input/output.
steps.resourceLimited: Use clinical hydration signs and timed urine checks where lab turnaround is delayed.

steps.title: Positioning and mobilization
steps.detail: Head-up position, early mobilization, and pain control to reduce ileus duration.
steps.resourceLimited: Encourage assisted turning and sitting if formal physiotherapy is unavailable.

pearls: Trend vitals and NG output together
topics.mnemonic: DRAIN: Distension, Review vitals, Assess NG, Investigate, Notify senior
topics.parentExplanation: After surgery, tummy swelling can happen, but we are checking carefully for blockage or infection so we can treat early.
topics.mentorTip: Do not anchor on ileus. Rising heart rate plus pain equals reassess for leak or sepsis.

topics.title: Blunt Abdominal Trauma
topics.description: Rapid trauma survey with hemorrhage prioritization.

decisionFlow.question: Is the child hypotensive or with altered perfusion?

decisionFlow.action: Activate massive transfusion protocol and urgent trauma surgery call.
Prepare for operative management with ongoing resuscitation.
Proceed to contrast CT and non-operative monitoring if stable.

steps.title: Airway and C-spine
steps.detail: Secure airway if compromised and maintain cervical spine precautions.
steps.resourceLimited: Prioritize jaw thrust, manual stabilization, and early transfer if advanced airway support is limited.

steps.title: Circulation
steps.detail: Two large-bore IV lines, blood products as needed, and frequent perfusion checks.
steps.resourceLimited: Use warmed crystalloid while arranging blood and monitor mental status and pulse pressure trends.

steps.title: Exposure and reassessment
steps.detail: Full exposure for injuries, then prevent hypothermia and reassess every 5-10 minutes.
steps.resourceLimited: Use warm blankets and serial focused exams when imaging access is delayed.

pearls: A stable child can deteriorate quickly after blunt trauma
pitfalls: Single early normal exam causing delay
topics.mnemonic: STOP: Stabilize, Triage bleeding, Operate when needed, Prevent hypothermia
topics.parentExplanation: We are checking for internal injury and bleeding. We will act quickly if surgery is needed and keep you updated at each step.
topics.mentorTip: In pediatric trauma, serial reassessment is as important as the first exam.
